evals.prompt: Run the pipeline integrity validation on the todo-api example
evals.expected_output: All checks pass: vision, personas, feature specs (Feature + Enabler types), journeys with AC cross-references, UX/UI designs, design system, manifest

assertions.description: At least one PASS in output

evals.prompt: Generate a health check endpoint 3 times with no vibomatic phases (just a one-line prompt) and 3 times with full pipeline (spec → tech design → code). Measure pairwise diff variance between the 3 outputs in each group.
evals.expected_output: Full-pipeline group has lower pairwise diff variance than no-phases group, demonstrating that progressive narrowing reduces implementation variance

evals.prompt: Take the todo-api feature spec and plant 5 known errors (contradicting AC, nonexistent file reference, ungrounded precondition, compound story, untestable AC). Run single-pass review and full review protocol. Count how many errors each approach finds.
evals.expected_output: Full review protocol finds more planted errors than single-pass review

assertions.description: Protocol finds at least as many errors
Protocol finds at least 4 of 5 planted errors
